chief_complaint: A 69-year-old male presented with a two-year history of progressive urinary retention, urgency, and frequency.
history: The patient had a two-year history of progressive urinary retention, urgency, and frequency. Initial management for benign prostatic hyperplasia was ineffective. He subsequently developed fever, headache, and dizziness. He had a history of untreated testicular tuberculosis and pulmonary nodules on CT.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) analysis revealed lymphocytic pleocytosis and elevated protein. CSF culture, Xpert MTB/RIF, and metagenomic next-generation sequencing (mNGS) were negative. MRI showed a prostatic nodule.
